summary.verdict_desc: 本周计数 5 红 / 8 黄 / 11 绿，红灯比例 20.8%（与上周持平），加权风险分由 35.4% 微升至 37.5%。红灯数未变，但水面之下广度再度走弱：S&P 500 50 日均线上方比例由 6/12 的约 61.2% 回落至 6/18 的 55.26%，自 60% 绿区跌回 40-60% 黄区，前一周的广度修复未能延续，breadth_50d 由绿转黄（本周唯一状态变化，方向偏风险）；与此同时 Top-5 权重由约 27% 抬升至约 28.8%（NVDA 7.9%+AAPL 6.8%+GOOGL 6.0%+MSFT 4.4%+AMZN 3.7%），集中度随广度走弱再度上行。市场情绪整体偏暖：NVDA FY27 Q1 录得创纪录营收 $81.6B（+85% YoY、数据中心 +92%），追加 $80B 回购、股息由 $0.01 提至 $0.25，并于 6/18 完成 $25B 发债；AI 板块本周企稳无系统性抛售，capex 指引反应由上周的'选择性惩罚'转向对兑现者的奖励，但市场仍要求高 capex 配业绩，维持黄灯。信用面继续宽松：HY 利差 6/12 收于约 271bps（远低于均值），CoreWeave 5 年期 CDS 由去年 12 月 8.81% 降至本月 4.52%、数据中心融资成本回落，Neocloud 信用零事件，全口径债务/Capex 比沿用上期约 54%（周期内无新拆解）。基本面持续兑现：OpenRouter token 量稳在约 100 万亿/月（半年 5 倍），Anthropic ARR 5 月约 $45-47B、二阶导仍正，Oracle RPO $638B。Fed 6/17 以 12-0 维持 3.50-3.75%、CPI 仍约 4.2%，higher-for-longer 延续。内部人持续净卖出（NVDA/PLTR/AVGO 滚动 12 月约 $4.6B、近乎零买入，本周取数恢复、解除上周 stale）。基础判读本在观察期（red_pct<25%），但分类强制升级再次生效：估值 2/3 红（CAPE ≈40.5、Top-5 ≈28.8%）叠加资金面 3/4 红（Mag4 FCF 深度负值、VC AI 占比 ~80%、NVDA 客户投资/收入 ~43%）。估值 + 资金面双类共振触发分类强制升级。
summary.verdict_desc_en: This week prints 5 red / 8 yellow / 11 green, with the red share at 20.8% (unchanged) and the weighted risk score edging up from 35.4% to 37.5%. The red count held, but beneath the surface breadth weakened again: the share of S&P 500 names above their 50-day average slipped from ~61.2% on June 12 to 55.26% on June 18, falling from the green zone back into the 40-60% yellow band — last week's breadth repair did not stick, flipping breadth_50d from green to yellow (the only status change this week, and a risk-ward one). At the same time the Top-5 weight rose from ~27% to ~28.8% (NVDA 7.9% + AAPL 6.8% + GOOGL 6.0% + MSFT 4.4% + AMZN 3.7%), with concentration climbing as breadth thinned. Sentiment stayed warm overall: NVDA posted record FY27 Q1 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY, data center +92%), added an $80B buyback, raised its dividend from $0.01 to $0.25, and completed a $25B debt sale on June 18; the AI complex steadied with no systematic selloff, and the capex-guidance reaction shifted from last week's 'selective punishment' toward rewarding those who deliver — though the market still demands capex be matched by results, keeping it yellow. Credit conditions stayed easy: HY OAS closed near 271bps on June 12 (well below average), CoreWeave's 5-year CDS fell to 4.52% this month from 8.81% last December as data-center funding costs eased, neocloud credit logged zero events, and the all-in Debt/Capex ratio carries the prior ~54% (no new bottom-up break this cycle). Fundamentals keep delivering: OpenRouter token volume holds at ~100T/month (5x in six months), Anthropic's ARR reached ~$45-47B in May with a still-positive second derivative, and Oracle's RPO sits at $638B. The Fed held 3.50-3.75% in a 12-0 vote on June 17 with CPI still ~4.2% as higher-for-longer persists. Insiders keep net-selling (NVDA/PLTR/AVGO ~$4.6B over the trailing 12 months with virtually zero buys; data sourcing recovered this week, clearing last week's stale flag). The base framework reads Observation (red_pct < 25%), but the category override fires again: valuation 2/3 RED (CAPE ≈40.5, Top-5 ≈28.8%) plus capital 3/4 RED (Mag4 FCF deeply negative, VC AI share ~80%, NVDA customer-financing ~43%). Valuation + capital dual-category resonance triggers the forced upgrade.

wow_changes.note: S&P 500 50 日均线上方比例由 6/12 的约 61.2% 回落至 6/18 的 55.26%，自 60% 绿线跌回 40-60% 黄区；上周的广度修复未能延续，涨势重新收窄向头部集中，转黄
wow_changes.note_en: The share of S&P 500 names above their 50-day average slipped from ~61.2% on June 12 to 55.26% on June 18, falling from the 60% green line back into the 40-60% yellow band; last week's breadth repair failed to hold as leadership re-narrowed toward mega-caps — flips yellow

wow_changes.from: 选择性惩罚（不上调指引者受罚，板块企稳）
wow_changes.to: 偏奖励（NVDA 创纪录财报领涨，板块无抛售）
wow_changes.note: NVDA FY27 Q1 营收创纪录 $81.6B（+85%）、追加 $80B 回购并提股息，6/18 完成 $25B 发债；本周 AI 板块企稳无系统性抛售，市场反应由上周'选择性惩罚'转向奖励兑现者，但仍要求高 capex 配业绩，维持黄灯
wow_changes.note_en: NVDA posted record FY27 Q1 revenue of $81.6B (+85%), added an $80B buyback and raised its dividend, and completed a $25B debt sale on June 18; the AI complex steadied with no systematic selloff and the reaction shifted from last week's 'selective punishment' toward rewarding deliverers, though the market still demands capex be matched by results — stays yellow

wow_changes.to: ~8x（本周取数恢复，确认持续净卖出）
wow_changes.note: 本周内部人交易数据恢复、解除上周 stale：NVDA/PLTR/AVGO 滚动 12 月累计卖出约 $4.6B、近乎零买入，Huang 一人约 $2.9B；卖买比仍偏高但未及 2000 年 23x 极值，维持黄灯
wow_changes.note_en: Insider-trade data sourcing recovered this week, clearing last week's stale flag: NVDA/PLTR/AVGO insiders sold ~$4.6B over the trailing 12 months with virtually zero purchases (Huang alone ~$2.9B); the sell/buy ratio stays elevated but below the 2000 extreme of 23x — stays yellow

indicators.note: CAPE 约 40.5（GuruFocus 40.43、multpl 41.71），仍是 155 年来第 2 高、距 2000 年峰值 44.2 仅约 8%；远高于长期均值、earnings yield 仅约 2.5%，整体估值处极度拉伸区，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: CAPE sits near 40.5 (GuruFocus 40.43, multpl 41.71), still the 2nd-highest in 155 years and only ~8% below the 2000 peak of 44.2; far above its long-run average with an earnings yield of just ~2.5% — overall valuations remain extremely stretched, stays red

indicators.note: S&P 500 前 5 大（NVDA 7.9% / AAPL 6.8% / GOOGL 6.0% / MSFT 4.4% / AMZN 3.7%）合计约 28.8%，较上周约 27% 再升；本周广度走弱令集中度回升，市值高度押注少数 AI 龙头，仍贴近 2000 年极值，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: The S&P 500's Top-5 (NVDA 7.9% / AAPL 6.8% / GOOGL 6.0% / MSFT 4.4% / AMZN 3.7%) total ~28.8%, up from ~27% a week ago; thinning breadth pushed concentration back up as the index bets heavily on a handful of AI leaders, still hugging the 2000 extreme — stays red

indicators.note: NVDA 远期 PE 约 23.4x（GuruFocus 23.45、FinanceCharts 23.42、StockAnalysis 21.2，区间 21-23.5x），远低于 12 个月均值约 43；FY27 Q1 营收创纪录 $81.6B（+85%）、EPS 持续上修使倍数维持历史低位，NVDA 自身估值并非泡沫源头，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA's forward PE is ~23.4x (GuruFocus 23.45, FinanceCharts 23.42, StockAnalysis 21.2; range 21-23.5x), far below its ~43 12-month average; record FY27 Q1 revenue of $81.6B (+85%) and ongoing EPS upgrades keep the multiple historically low — NVDA's own valuation is not the bubble's source, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 指引下调=红 / 加速=黄 / 稳健=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Guidance cut = red / Accelerating = yellow / Steady = green
indicators.note: 四大 hyperscaler 2026 capex 合计约 $725B（AMZN $200B、MSFT $190B、GOOGL $175-185B、META $115-135B），YoY +77%，无一下调指引；内存涨价推升成本，capex 已占经营现金流约 94%、需外部融资支撑；继续加速、不可持续特征明显，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: The four hyperscalers guide ~$725B of 2026 capex (AMZN $200B, MSFT $190B, GOOGL $175-185B, META $115-135B), +77% YoY, with none cutting guidance; memory inflation lifts costs and capex now absorbs ~94% of operating cash flow, leaning on external financing — still accelerating with clear unsustainability hallmarks, stays yellow

indicators.note: Amazon 尾随 FCF 由约 $26B 暴跌至约 $1.2B、2026 全年预计转负约 -$17B 至 -$28B（capex $200B、经营现金流约 $139.5B）；Mag4 合计 FCF 料降至多年低位，仅 MSFT 仍正增长，capex 持续吞噬现金流，无季度新数据沿用上季口径，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: Amazon's trailing FCF plunged from ~$26B to ~$1.2B and is projected to turn negative ~-$17B to -$28B for full-year 2026 ($200B capex vs ~$139.5B operating cash flow); combined Mag4 FCF is set to multi-year lows with only MSFT still growing as capex keeps devouring cash; no new quarterly print, carrying the prior read — stays red

indicators.name_zh: AI 占 VC 投资比重
indicators.note: Crunchbase/PitchBook Q1 2026：全球 VC 创纪录 $297-300B，AI 占 80-81%（$242-255B），较 2025 Q1 的 55% 大幅跃升；OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI 三笔大单占 67%、美元高度集中于少数巨额轮次，无季度新数据沿用 Q1 口径，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: Crunchbase/PitchBook Q1 2026: global VC hit a record $297-300B with AI taking 80-81% ($242-255B), up sharply from 55% in Q1 2025; OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI alone made up 67% as dollars concentrate in a few mega-rounds; no new quarterly print, carrying the Q1 read — stays red

indicators.note: NVDA 2026 年至今已承诺 AI 股权投资 $40B+（含 OpenAI $30B 直投、由 $100B 意向缩减，xAI、CoreWeave $2B 等），累计承诺 $110B+ 再加 GPU 抵押债务；对 LTM 收入约 $300B（FY27 Q1 单季 $81.6B）的比率约 43%，远超 Lucent 1999 峰值 24%，每笔投资捆绑采购协议，循环融资特征明显，维持红灯
indicators.note_en: NVDA has committed $40B+ of AI equity so far in 2026 (incl. a $30B direct OpenAI stake trimmed from a $100B LOI, plus xAI and a $2B CoreWeave stake), with cumulative commitments topping $110B plus GPU-backed debt; against ~$300B LTM revenue (FY27 Q1 alone $81.6B) that is ~43%, far above Lucent's 1999 peak of 24%, with every deal bundled to a purchase agreement — circular-financing hallmarks persist, stays red

indicators.note: S5FI 6/18 收于约 55.26%（6/12 为 61.2%），自 60% 绿线回落至 40-60% 黄区；上周的广度修复未能延续，涨势重新向头部收窄、参与度转弱，由绿转黄
indicators.note_en: S5FI closed near 55.26% on June 18 (61.2% on June 12), pulling back from the 60% green line into the 40-60% yellow band; last week's breadth repair failed to hold as leadership re-narrowed toward mega-caps and participation softened — flips yellow

indicators.name_zh: 市值加权 vs 等权重
indicators.note: 等权重 RSP 继续领先市值加权 SPY：RSP YTD +9.67% vs SPY +8.38%，收益差约 -1.3% 仍落在 <5% 绿区；过去一月 RSP +2.49%、SPY 几近持平，广度在涨势层面仍偏均衡，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Equal-weight RSP keeps leading cap-weighted SPY: RSP +9.67% YTD vs SPY +8.38%, a ~-1.3% spread still inside the sub-5% green zone; over the past month RSP gained 2.49% while SPY was roughly flat, so return breadth stays balanced — stays green

indicators.name_zh: AI 龙头内部人卖买比
indicators.note: 本周取数恢复：NVDA/PLTR/AVGO 内部人滚动 12 月累计卖出约 $4.6B、近乎零买入（Huang 一人约 $2.9B，6/6 董事 Mark Stevens 售 $221M）；个人层面仍以卖出为主、卖买比偏高但未及 2000 年 23x 极值，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Data sourcing recovered this week: NVDA/PLTR/AVGO insiders sold ~$4.6B over the trailing 12 months with virtually zero purchases (Huang alone ~$2.9B; director Mark Stevens sold $221M on June 6); individual flows stay sell-heavy and the ratio is elevated but below the 2000 extreme of 23x — stays yellow

indicators.threshold_text: 洪流=红 / 升温=黄 / 平静=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Flood = red / Warming = yellow / Quiet = green
indicators.note: SpaceX 6/12 完成史上最大 IPO 后，管道继续升温但未成洪流：OpenAI 6/8 已递交保密 S-1（拟估值约 $1T）、Anthropic 拟最早 10 月上市（估值约 $900B）、Databricks 料 Q3 递表；已挂牌/临近发行的具体公司数仍 <10，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: After SpaceX completed the largest IPO ever on June 12, the pipeline keeps warming but is short of a Flood: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 on June 8 (~$1T target), Anthropic eyes a listing as early as October (~$900B), and Databricks is expected to file in Q3; the count of listed/imminent names is still under 10 — stays yellow

indicators.note: ICE BofA HY OAS 6/12 约 2.71%（271 bps），远低于长期均值 3.94%、中位数 4.52%；尽管 AI 基建 $725B capex 持续烧钱，信用市场对 AI 风险仍未现压力，是当前最支撑多头的指标，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: ICE BofA HY OAS was ~2.71% (271 bps) on June 12, far below the 3.94% long-run average and 4.52% median; despite the ongoing $725B AI-infrastructure cash burn, credit markets still show no stress on AI risk — the most bull-supportive indicator right now, stays green

indicators.name_zh: 数据中心 ABS 利差
indicators.threshold_text: 走阔 50bps+ = 红 / 稳定 = 黄 / 收窄 = 绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widening 50bps+ = red / Stable = yellow / Tightening = green
indicators.note: 数据中心融资成本继续回落：CoreWeave 5 年期 CDS 由去年 12 月 8.81% 降至本月约 4.52%，新发无担保票据约 8.9%、DDTL 持有成本由 15% 降至 5.9%；数据中心 ABS/高收益债发行（2026 年逾 $30B）条件持续宽松，信用市场对 AI 基建仍未现压力，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Data-center funding costs keep easing: CoreWeave's 5-year CDS fell to ~4.52% this month from 8.81% last December, new unsecured notes price near 8.9%, and DDTL carry dropped from 15% to 5.9%; data-center ABS/high-yield issuance (over $30B in 2026) remains accommodative with no credit stress on AI infrastructure — stays green

indicators.note: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda 90 日内无评级下调或违约；CoreWeave 信用持续回暖（CDS 由 8.81% 降至 4.52%）、再融资渠道畅通，$99.4B 积压多为 take-or-pay；其总债务高企、客户与到期集中仍是潜在风险，但短期信用零事件，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda — no downgrades or defaults in 90 days; CoreWeave's credit keeps recovering (CDS down to 4.52% from 8.81%) with open refinancing channels and a $99.4B mostly take-or-pay backlog; high total debt and concentrated maturities remain a latent risk, but with zero near-term events it stays green

indicators.name_zh: 全口径债务/Capex 流量比
indicators.note: 自下而上拆解（流量口径）：分子=2026E 全口径 AI 新增债务，Morgan Stanley 估全年约 $570B（含 hyperscaler 及 JV $250-300B、neoclouds 与 Meta Hyperion/Oracle Stargate 等表外 SPV）；分母=2026E 全口径 AI 基建 capex 约 $1.0-1.1T（五大 hyperscaler 含 Oracle ~$805B + neoclouds/其他）。$570B ÷ ~$1.05T ≈ 54%（区间 48-60%），仍低于互联网/电信基建泡沫顶 80-100%，列中度警戒（2026-06-21 周期内无新数据，沿用 2026-06-14 拆解）。
indicators.note_en: Bottom-up, flow basis: numerator = 2026E all-in new AI debt, Morgan Stanley estimates ~$570B for the year (incl. $250-300B of hyperscaler + JV issuance and off-balance-sheet SPVs such as Meta Hyperion and Oracle Stargate); denominator = 2026E all-in AI-infrastructure capex ~$1.0-1.1T (the five hyperscalers incl. Oracle ~$805B plus neoclouds/others). ~$570B ÷ ~$1.05T ≈ 54% (range 48-60%), still below the 80-100% peak of the dot-com / telecom build-outs — scored moderate (no new data within the cycle as of 2026-06-21, carrying the 2026-06-14 breakdown).
indicators.source_name: Morgan Stanley + 自下而上拆解

indicators.name_zh: AI 行业 Token 月度环比
indicators.threshold_text: 收缩=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Contraction = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: OpenRouter 月 token 量稳在约 100 万亿（周 25 万亿、半年 5 倍），单日已超 1 万亿；服务超 800 万用户、接入 400+ 模型，全产业用量继续加速，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: OpenRouter holds at ~100T tokens/month (25T weekly, 5x in six months), already processing 1T+ per day; serving 8M+ users across 400+ models, industry-wide usage keeps accelerating — stays green

indicators.name_zh: Token 增速 / 收入增速 比值
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR 由 4 月约 $30B 升至 5 月约 $45-47B（环比约 +50%），收入增速持续快于 token 量增速（半年 5 倍、月环比约 +35%）；单 token 价值上行、付费需求真实，比值维持 <1x，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Anthropic's ARR rose from ~$30B in April to ~$45-47B in May (~+50% MoM), with revenue growth still outpacing token-volume growth (5x in six months, ~+35% MoM); per-token value keeps rising on genuine paid demand, holding the ratio below 1x — stays green

indicators.name_zh: AI 收入增速的二阶导
indicators.threshold_text: 减速=红 / 平稳=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Decel = red / Flat = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Anthropic run-rate 由 4 月约 $30B 升至 5 月约 $45-47B，月增量持续放大、约 6 周翻倍；已领先 OpenAI ~$33B，二阶导维持正向；唯 OpenAI 仍对其毛收入口径有约 $8B 争议，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Anthropic's run-rate climbed from ~$30B in April to ~$45-47B in May, with the monthly increment still expanding (doubling roughly every six weeks) and now ahead of OpenAI's ~$33B — the 2nd derivative stays positive, though OpenAI still disputes ~$8B of its gross-revenue accounting; stays green

indicators.note: McKinsey Q1 2026：72% 企业至少 1 项 AI workload 生产化（2024 55%、2020 20%）；Deloitte：67% 企业月耗 token 超 10 亿、25% 已将 ≥40% 实验转产；深度集成仍在爬坡但整体高位，无季度新调查沿用，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: McKinsey Q1 2026: 72% of enterprises run at least one AI workload in production (vs 55% in 2024, 20% in 2020); Deloitte: 67% now use 1B+ tokens monthly and 25% have moved 40%+ of experiments to production — deep integration still climbing but the headline stays high; no new quarterly survey, carried over — stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 负增长=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Negative = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Oracle FY26 Q4（6/10）RPO $638B（+363% YoY、单季 +$85B），全年由 Q1 $455B→Q2 $523B→Q3 $553B→Q4 $638B 逐季抬升；MSFT 商业 RPO 约为 Oracle 2.4 倍、GCP 积压持续扩张，合计积压超 $1.4T，递延需求强劲；但 Oracle 逾 50% RPO 来自 OpenAI、$75B 为预付/客户供硬件，强需求与客户集中并存，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: Oracle's FY26 Q4 (June 10) RPO hit $638B (+363% YoY, +$85B sequentially), rising each quarter through the year ($455B→$523B→$553B→$638B); MSFT's commercial RPO is ~2.4x Oracle's and GCP's backlog keeps expanding, bringing the combined pipeline above $1.4T as deferred demand stays robust; but over 50% of Oracle's RPO is from OpenAI and $75B is prepaid/customer-supplied hardware — strong demand alongside concentration, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 任何=红 / 调查=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any confirmed = red / Investigation = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 无主要 AI 龙头新增正式执法；Super Micro 3/19 三名关联人被控出口管制违规（非会计造假、公司未被列为被告），本周宣布 $7B 融资；Burry 仍指控 Mag7 借延长服务器折旧虚增利润，但均非核心 AI 龙头执法，本周无新增，维持绿灯
indicators.note_en: No new formal enforcement against a major AI bellwether; Super Micro saw three associated individuals charged on March 19 over export-control violations (not accounting fraud, with the company not named) and announced $7B of financing this week; Burry keeps alleging Mag7 inflates profits via extended server depreciation, but none are core AI-leader enforcement actions — no new events this week, stays green

indicators.threshold_text: 加息=红 / 通胀压力=黄 / 降息=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Hike = red / Inflation pressure = yellow / Cut = green
indicators.note: FOMC 6/17 以 12-0 维持利率 3.50-3.75%（CME 此前给出维持概率约 96%）；CPI 仍约 4.2% 远超 2% 目标、劳动力稳健，higher-for-longer 延续，对极度拉伸的估值构成持续压制，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: The FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% in a 12-0 vote on June 17 (CME had priced ~96% hold odds); CPI still runs ~4.2%, far above the 2% target, with a firm labor market — higher-for-longer persists, a continued headwind for extremely stretched valuations, stays yellow

indicators.name_zh: 资本开支指引市场反应
indicators.threshold_text: 系统性惩罚=红 / 偶发=黄 / 奖励=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Systematic punishment = red / Episodic = yellow / Rewarded = green
indicators.note: 本周市场情绪转暖：NVDA FY27 Q1 创纪录营收 $81.6B（+85%）、追加 $80B 回购并提股息领涨，AI 板块企稳无系统性抛售，反应由上周对'不上调指引者'的选择性惩罚转向奖励兑现者；但市场仍要求高 capex 配业绩、上调指引未获普遍奖赏，尚未稳定回到奖励区，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Sentiment warmed this week: NVDA's record FY27 Q1 revenue of $81.6B (+85%), an added $80B buyback and a dividend hike led the tape higher, the AI complex steadied with no systematic selloff, and the reaction shifted from last week's selective punishment of non-raisers toward rewarding deliverers; but the market still demands capex be matched by results and raised guidance is not yet broadly rewarded, so it has not stably returned to the reward zone — stays yellow

indicators.threshold_text: 普遍承认过热=红 / 部分=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widespread acknowledgment = red / Partial = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 对冲表态持续：Altman 直言小盘 AI 估值'非理性、insane'、'必有人巨亏'；Dimon 称'不少钱很可能会亏'、未来两年股市大跌概率高于市场定价；Burry 警告当前已似互联网泡沫末期；但 Pichai 等仍强调 AI 属代际级技术、CEO 未集体收手，未达全体一致承认过热，维持黄灯
indicators.note_en: Hedging persists: Altman calls small-cap AI valuations 'irrational, insane' and warns 'someone will lose a phenomenal amount'; Dimon says 'a lot of the money will probably be lost' with above-market odds of a meaningful drawdown over two years; Burry likens current conditions to the late dot-com bubble; yet Pichai and others still frame AI as a generational technology and CEOs have not collectively pulled back — short of a universal 'overheated' consensus, stays yellow
